text.title: solution-assessment Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for solution-assessment as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: No baseline — cannot measure improvement. "Revenue went up after deployment" without a baseline is not a business value assessment.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Lock baselines before development starts using the same query and data source that will be used at assessment time.

failure-mode.description: Survey-only assessment under-samples churners — surveys reach users who still use the solution; the ones who churned never answer.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Reconcile survey n with active user count; flag if response rate is below 30% or if churned users are absent from the panel.

failure-mode.description: One-shot assessment — a single 30-day report misses the honeymoon effect; real friction shows at 90+ days.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Run assessments at 30, 60, and 90 days; compare the three reports. A metric that was on-track at 30 and at-risk at 60 is the real story.

failure-mode.description: Assessor reports to the project sponsor whose bonus depends on the result — independence is compromised.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Route assessment through a different reporting chain or external reviewer. Independence is what gives the report weight at the steering committee.
